fields.helpText: The name assigned to this particular communications protocol.
The executable MUMPS code that will send the data in the variable XMSG.
The executable MUMPS code which will receive data and store it in the variable XMRG.

The executable MUMPS code which will open the communication channel, setting the variable IO to the proper value.   There are a couple of variables that can be set in this area to control  the connection:   XMNO220=1 will stop MailMan from issuing its second (and mandatory when talking to another MailMan system, but fatal when talking to non-MailMan SMTP messaging systems) 220 'ready' when the receiver is indicating it is on the line and ready to receive.   XMD="D" causes MailMan to echo on the
The executable MUMPS code which will close the communication channel.
